behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: rename detection comes from real `git` subprocess output/flags. MOCK-HIDE risk is high: mocked diff/status text can miss failures where the tool invokes git without rename detection (or parses real rename records incorrectly). Failure boundary is CLI↔git/OS interaction, beyond a single function.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, then run the CLI against that repo. Assert report shows `renamed=1` and does not increment `added` or `deleted` for that same change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: binary diff behavior is produced by real git subprocess and file content handling. MOCK-HIDE is significant: mocked text often omits binary-specific output/edge formats, so crashes in real parsing paths can be missed. This is not LLM-dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit an initial binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify it, and run the CLI. Assert process exits successfully and the binary change is reflected in counts (category expected by the tool’s schema).

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: unborn-branch/no-commit repos trigger specific real git error modes. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing git responses can hide command/exit-code handling bugs that only occur with real subprocess behavior. Failure boundary is CLI process + git state.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a temporary git repo with no commits, run the CLI, and assert successful exit plus an empty structured report (zero counts/no items) instead of traceback or fatal error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Although counting logic could be unit-tested, this behavior’s real failure boundary includes end-to-end history extraction from git over real commits, renames, deletions, and modifications. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: fixture-only tests can pass while command selection/revision-range handling is wrong in real repos.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temporary repo history with known operations (add, modify, rename, delete across multiple commits), run the CLI for the target range, and assert each category count exactly matches the constructed ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: staged vs unstaged semantics rely on real git index/worktree behavior. MOCK-HIDE is high because mocked repository state often flattens these distinctions and misses invocation bugs (wrong diff target/index usage). Not agentic.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary real repo, create equivalent file edits in two scenarios: (A) edits staged, (B) edits unstaged. Run the CLI in each scenario with identical analysis scope and assert the structured stdout reports are identical.
